title: My Feelings & Emotions: English-Spanish Vocabulary Pack
description: An engaging vocabulary pack designed for K-2 ELL newcomers to learn and identify basic feelings and emotions in English and Spanish, featuring a mini-book and interactive worksheets.

vocabulary.image_prompt: A child smiling brightly, maybe jumping or with hands up in joy.
A child with tears or a frown, looking down.
A child with a red face, furrowed brows, perhaps stomping a foot.
A child with wide eyes, hands near face or hiding behind something.
A child with an open mouth and wide eyes, hands to cheeks.
A child peeking from behind hands or an object, looking down.
A child standing tall, smiling confidently, perhaps holding a certificate or drawing.
A child with a big smile, hands up in the air, full of energy.
A child sitting peacefully, perhaps taking a deep breath or meditating.
A child making a funny face, sticking out their tongue, or crossing eyes.

worksheets.instructions_en: Match the English feeling word to the correct picture. Draw a line.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Une la palabra en inglés con la imagen correcta. Dibuja una línea.

items.image_prompt: A child with a frown and tears.
A child with a red face and furrowed brows.
A child with wide eyes, hands near face.
A child with an open mouth and wide eyes.

worksheets.instructions_l2: Traza las palabras de los sentimientos.

worksheets.instructions_en: Color the pictures that show different feelings.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Colorea las imágenes que muestran diferentes sentimientos.

items.image_prompt: A simple cartoon face with a big smile.
A simple cartoon face with a frown and a tear.
A simple cartoon face with furrowed brows and a scowl.
A simple cartoon face with wide eyes and an open mouth.
A simple cartoon face sticking out its tongue.

mini_book.title: How Do You Feel? / ¿Cómo te sientes?

pages.image_prompt: A title page with various emoji-like faces showing different feelings.
A child smiling brightly with sun rays around them.
A child with a tear, sitting with head down.
A child with red cheeks and stomping foot.
A child hiding behind a blanket or hands, wide eyes.
A child with wide eyes and open mouth looking at a gift.
A child sitting cross-legged, taking a deep breath.
A child looking at themselves in a mirror, thinking about their feelings.

teacher_notes.objective: Students will be able to identify and name basic feelings and emotions in English and Spanish. They will use simple sentences to express how they feel.
teacher_notes.suggested_use: Introduce vocabulary using flashcards or realia (emojis, facial expressions in a mirror). Read the mini-book aloud, pausing to discuss each feeling. Complete the worksheets as follow-up activities. Encourage students to express their feelings daily in English and Spanish during morning circle time or check-ins.
differentiation_tips: **Support:** Provide sentence frames ('I feel _____', 'Me siento _____'). Use gesture and total physical response (TPR) to act out feelings. Pair students with a strong L1 speaker or an English-speaking buddy. Use real-life scenarios to discuss feelings.

**Challenge:** Ask students to describe *why* they feel a certain way. Encourage them to use feeling words in longer sentences or to write a short story about a character's feelings. Have them create their own feeling faces or scenarios.

answer_key.answers: No specific answer key needed as it's a creative coloring activity; focus on effort and engagement.
